skill.name: code-review-assistant
skill.description: Coordinates pull request reviews across security, performance, style, test, and documentation checks, then prepares review feedback and fix suggestions.
skill.summary: Runs structured pull request reviews with specialist quality checks and merge readiness guidance.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified extensive shell and filesystem usage in the documented execution flow, but I found no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection. The skill should be published with a warning because it can run GitHub, npx, claude-flow, and Codex commands, create local review artifacts, check out PR code, and post PR review comments.

medium_findings.title: External Review Commands Execute Against Repository Content
medium_findings.description: The execution flow runs GitHub CLI, npx claude-flow, and Codex commands against PR content. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it can execute local tooling, checkout untrusted branches, and depend on installed command behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell workflow explicitly invokes external CLIs for review automation. The intent is legitimate code review, but the commands operate on repository and PR content.

medium_findings.title: GitHub Pull Request Side Effects
medium_findings.description: The workflow posts review comments and can approve or request changes on a pull request. This is expected behavior, but it can affect repository collaboration state if run without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands directly call GitHub review and comment actions. The risk is clear because these actions write to a remote pull request.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Windows SAM Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography and Windows SAM database alerts match ordinary review text and variable names such as security scoring. I found no code that reads Windows credential stores or implements weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context shows descriptive metadata, score variables, and failure-mode text. No evidence found for credential database access, cryptographic implementation, or reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Benign Shell Substitution and Device Redirection
low_findings.description: Many static shell-substitution and filesystem alerts are normal Bash scripting patterns for reading generated review files, calculating scores, and suppressing missing-file errors with standard error redirection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell substitutions consume local review artifacts created earlier in the workflow. These are not hidden exfiltration or destructive filesystem operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell-Based Automation Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a Bash workflow that runs multiple external commands and writes review artifacts. Users should run it only in trusted repositories or isolated environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash block is explicit and spans the core execution flow. It is a legitimate automation pattern with elevated operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote GitHub Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow can comment on, approve, or request changes on a pull request through GitHub CLI commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and flags are directly visible and perform remote PR state changes. No inference is needed beyond standard GitHub CLI behavior.

content.user_title: Review Pull Requests with Specialist Agents
content.value_statement: Manual pull request review can miss security, test, and maintainability issues. This skill coordinates focused review passes and produces merge readiness guidance.
actual_capabilities: Coordinates security, performance, style, test, and documentation review areas.
Collects GitHub pull request metadata and changed file information.
Runs documented quality audit commands through external review tooling.
Aggregates review findings into a structured pull request summary.
Generates fix suggestions when review tools report issues.
Posts pull request feedback and merge recommendations through GitHub CLI.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI authentication and repository access.
Depends on claude-flow, Codex, jq, bc, and related command-line tools.
May check out and inspect untrusted pull request code.
Automated scores still require human review before merging important changes.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Run a broad review before maintainers decide whether a pull request is ready to merge.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Change Review
use_cases.description: Focus review effort on secrets, unsafe patterns, and critical security findings in a proposed change.

use_cases.title: Quality Gate Automation
use_cases.description: Produce repeatable review summaries that combine test, style, documentation, and quality signals.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Pull Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review pull request 123 with all default focus areas. Summarize blockers, warnings, and merge readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Security and Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Review pull request 123 with focus areas security and tests. Include fix suggestions for blocking issues.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Merge Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changed files in this branch and decide whether the pull request is ready to merge. Explain any required changes.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Full Quality Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across multiple quality dimensions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete pull request review using security, performance, style, tests, and documentation checks. Prepare a GitHub review comment and list approval criteria.

output_examples.input: Review a feature pull request before merge.
output_examples.output: A ranked review summary with blocking issues, warnings, suggestions, category scores, and a merge recommendation.

output_examples.input: Review only security and test quality for a pull request.
output_examples.output: A focused report covering potential vulnerabilities, missing tests, failed checks, and suggested remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Prepare maintainers for a release branch merge.
output_examples.output: A merge readiness assessment with quality gate status, remaining risks, and recommended reviewer actions.

best_practices: Run the workflow in a clean checkout with authenticated GitHub CLI access.
Review generated approvals and comments before posting them to important repositories.
Use focused review areas when a pull request changes sensitive code paths.
anti_patterns: Do not run the workflow on untrusted code without isolation.
Do not enable automatic merge behavior without repository policy checks.
Do not treat generated scores as a replacement for owner review.

faq.question: Does this skill modify pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow can post comments and submit GitHub reviews when those commands are run.

faq.question: Which tools does it require?
faq.answer: It expects GitHub CLI, claude-flow commands, Codex, jq, bc, and shell access.

faq.question: Can it review only security issues?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow supports focus areas such as security, performance, style, tests, and documentation.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted pull requests?
faq.answer: Use isolation for untrusted code because the workflow checks out PR branches and runs local review tooling.

faq.question: Does it replace human reviewers?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured findings and recommendations that humans should verify.

faq.question: Can it generate fix suggestions?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow can ask Codex to suggest fixes based on aggregated review findings.
